cases.description: Verify onboarding popup, camera access and photo capture
steps: 2,.Verify the Splash Screen displays the HP logo with the "sprocket" text.
3.Wait for the Splash Screen to complete automatically.
4.Swipe through all onboarding screens until the final screen.
7.Wait until the Home screen is displayed.
8.If the notification permission popup appears, tap Allow.
10.Verify the Photobooth onboarding popup appears the first time the module is opened.
12.Verify the camera permission popup "Allow Sprocket to take pictures and record video?" is displayed.

13.Verify the permission options are displayed:While using the app
.Only this time
.Don't allow
.Tap While using the app.
14.Verify the Photobooth Camera screen opens successfully.
15.Verify the Flash icon is OFF by default.
16.Verify the Front Camera is selected by default.
17.Verify the Switch Camera icon is displayed.
18.Verify the Shutter button is displayed at the bottom of the screen.
19.Tap the Flash icon and verify it toggles ON and OFF.
20.Tap the Switch Camera icon and verify it switches between the Front and Back cameras.
21.Tap the Shutter button.
22.Verify a 3-second countdown is displayed before each capture.
23.Verify the application automatically captures 4 photos, with a 3-second countdown between each shot.
24.Verify the captured photos are displayed successfully.
25.Tap the Back button.
26.Verify the app returns to the previous screen successfully without any errors.
cases.expected: Onboarding popup is displayed successfully. Camera permission is granted and the Photobooth Camera screen opens successfully. Flash is OFF by default and Front Camera is selected by default. Flash and Camera Switch icons work correctly. The app captures 4 photos with a 3-second countdown between each shot. Back button returns the user to the Home screen successfully.

verifications.description: Verify onboarding popup, camera access and photo capture
verifications.expected: Onboarding popup is displayed successfully. Camera permission is granted and the Photobooth Camera screen opens successfully. Flash is OFF by default and Front Camera is selected by default. Flash and Camera Switch icons work correctly. The app captures 4 photos with a 3-second countdown between each shot. Back button returns the user to the Home screen successfully.
verifications.verification: Manual/heuristic check — review expected result on current screen

cases.description: Verify behavior when camera permission is denied
steps: 1. Launch the app and wait for the splash screen.
3. Tap Get Started! and on the Sign Up screen tap  " Sign Up Later".
6. Verify the onboarding popup appears for the first time.
7. Tap Don't Allow on the camera permission popup.
8. Tap the Shutter button or camera controls.
cases.expected: Camera permission is denied and the Photobooth Camera screen does not open. An appropriate permission/error message is displayed. User cannot capture photos or use camera controls until permission is granted. The app remains stable without any crash or UI issue.

verifications.description: Verify behavior when camera permission is denied
verifications.expected: Camera permission is denied and the Photobooth Camera screen does not open. An appropriate permission/error message is displayed. User cannot capture photos or use camera controls until permission is granted. The app remains stable without any crash or UI issue.
verifications.verification: Manual/heuristic check — review expected result on current screen

cases.description: Verify shutter to stop button transformation and countdown
steps: 1. Launch the app and wait for the splash screen.
3. Tap Get Started! and on the Sign Up screen tap "Sign Up  Later".
6. Verify the onboarding popup appears for the first time.
8. Verify the Photobooth Camera screen opens successfully.
9. Verify Flash is OFF and Front Camera is selected by default.
11. Verify the round shutter button changes to a square Stop button.
12. Verify the countdown displays 3 → 2 → 1 before each photo.
13. Verify Back, Flash, and Switch Camera buttons disappear during capture.
14. Verify the app captures 4 photos successfully.
cases.expected: Round shutter button changes to a square Stop button instantly. Countdown numbers 3, 2, 1 are displayed clearly before each photo. Flash, Switch Camera, and Back buttons are hidden during capture. The app captures 4 photos successfully without any issue.

verifications.description: Verify shutter to stop button transformation and countdown
verifications.expected: Round shutter button changes to a square Stop button instantly. Countdown numbers 3, 2, 1 are displayed clearly before each photo. Flash, Switch Camera, and Back buttons are hidden during capture. The app captures 4 photos successfully without any issue.
verifications.verification: Manual/heuristic check — review expected result on current screen

cases.description: Verify cancel photo session during countdown
steps: 1. Launch the app and wait for the splash screen.
3. Tap Get Started! and on the Sign Up screen tap "Sign Up Later".
6. Verify the onboarding popup appears for the first time.
8. Verify the Photobooth Camera screen opens successfully.
9. Tap the round Shutter button to start capturing.
10. During the countdown, tap the square Stop button.
cases.expected: Photo session is cancelled immediately. Any photos captured during the session are discarded and not saved. User remains on the Photobooth camera screen without any crash or UI issue.

verifications.description: Verify cancel photo session during countdown
verifications.expected: Photo session is cancelled immediately. Any photos captured during the session are discarded and not saved. User remains on the Photobooth camera screen without any crash or UI issue.
verifications.verification: Manual/heuristic check — review expected result on current screen

cases.description: Verify countdown hides during photo capture
steps:
1. Launch the app and wait for the Splash screen. 
2. Complete onboarding (ON_01B to ON_01E). 
3. Tap Get Started! and on the Sign Up screen tap "Sign Up Later". 
4. Verify the Home screen appears. 
5. Tap the Photobooth module.
 6. Verify onboarding popup appears for the first time. 
7. Verify popup text and buttons are displayed correctly. 
8. Allow camera permission by tapping "While using the app". 9. Verify the Photobooth Camera screen opens successfully. 10. Verify top options: Back button and title are visible. 
11. Verify bottom options: Flash, Shutter, and Switch Camera icons are visible. 
12. Verify Flash is OFF and Front Camera is selected by default. 13. Tap the Shutter button. 
14. Let the countdown reach 1 and observe the photo capture moment.
cases.expected: Countdown number disappears immediately when the photo is captured and the photo is saved successfully.

verifications.description: Verify countdown hides during photo capture
verifications.expected: Countdown number disappears immediately when the photo is captured and the photo is saved successfully.
verifications.verification: Manual/heuristic check — review expected result on current screen

cases.description: Verify progress indicator updates after each photo
steps: 1. Perform Steps 1 to 13 of TC_PB_01. 2. Complete the first photo capture. 3. Observe the progress indicator at the bottom of the screen. 4. Continue the capture sequence and observe progress updates.
cases.expected: Progress indicator updates correctly and shows captured photos count out of 4.

verifications.description: Verify progress indicator updates after each photo

cases.description: Verify Photo ID onboarding popup UI
steps: 2. Verify the Splash Screen displays the HP logo with the "sprocket" text.
3. Wait for the Splash Screen to complete automatically.
4. Swipe through all onboarding screens until the final screen.
6. Tap the Sign Up Later button.
7. Wait until the Home screen is displayed.
8. If the notification permission popup appears, tap Allow.
10. Verify the camera permission popup "Allow Sprocket to take pictures and record video?" is displayed.
11. Verify the permission options are displayed:

12. Tap While using the app. 
13Verify the Photo ID Mode popup is displayed (only the first time the Photo ID module is opened).
14.Verify the Photo ID icon is displayed.
15.Verify the title "Photo ID Mode" is displayed.

16.Verify the description is displayed:

17."Sprocket Photo ID mode provides tips to take a formal photo. 
18We don't guarantee that the photo will be accepted by all entities that require an official photo. 19.You will need to confirm that the photo meets the applicable requirements."

20.Verify the OK button is displayed and is selected by default.
21.Verify the Don't Show Again option is displayed below the OK button.
22.Tap the OK button.
23.Verify the Photo ID Camera screen opens successfully.
24.Verify the Photo ID icon is displayed on the camera screen.
25.Verify the Background controls are disabled by default.
26.Verify the user can continue using the Photo ID camera without any errors.
cases.expected: Popup UI is displayed correctly and background controls are disabled.

verifications.description: Verify Photo ID onboarding popup UI
verifications.expected: Popup UI is displayed correctly and background controls are disabled.
